acceptanceReviewed.criterion: Repo does not treat the grim-based encode sample as no-CPU-readback evidence.
acceptanceReviewed.evidence: m2-video-path-validation notes and bundle-manifest explicitly record the no-CPU-readback-after-grim non-claim; m2-nvenc-encode-sample notes now state that the materialized PNG/RGB artifacts do not prove no CPU readback.

acceptanceReviewed.criterion: Repo does not treat the grim-based encode sample as zero-copy evidence.
acceptanceReviewed.evidence: m2-video-path-validation bundle-manifest records direct DMA-BUF import as an unproven non-claim and its known finding says no zero-copy claim should be made; m2-nvenc-encode-sample notes now state that the materialized PNG/RGB artifacts do not prove zero-copy capture or direct capture-to-NVENC DMA-BUF transfer.

acceptanceReviewed.criterion: Node-specific evidence and qd reports are present.

rg and jq checks passed; nix develop -c just verify also passed.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node verifies evidence wording and classification only; it does not attempt live capture or no-CPU-readback validation.

resolvedFindings.finding: m2-video-path-validation does not prove no CPU readback after grim capture
resolvedFindings.resolution: Verified as an explicit non-claim, not a positive evidence claim. Added narrow encode-sample note clarification so both upstream and bundled evidence directories state the materialized diagnostic sample limitation.

residualRisks: This node verifies wording and evidence classification only. It does not attempt to prove no CPU readback, zero-copy capture, or direct capture-to-NVENC DMA-BUF transfer.
